meta.tagline: A faithful iPod interface rebuilt for the web. Custom state navigation, multi-modal click wheel and Spotify integration.

overview.heading: What is SpotiPod?
overview.intro: SpotiPod is a web app that reimagines the classic iPod interface, rebuilt from scratch with a custom state-based navigation system and full Spotify integration.
overview.body: The click wheel supports touch, mouse drag, keyboard, and screen reader input. The iPod shell is rendered in SVG with a dynamic overlay theming system. Spotify is connected via Authorization Code Flow with PKCE. Social playlist discovery runs on a Neon PostgreSQL database.

systems.title: Navigation System
systems.summary: A fully custom state-based menu system built from scratch. Tracks position, history, and transitions across the entire app — no framework router.
systems.detail: Every menu item is assigned a state type — new view, playback action, or nested menu. History is persisted across pages so the system always remembers where the user came from. Smooth transitions and back-navigation work identically to a real iPod.

systems.title: Click Wheel
systems.summary: A faithful digital translation of the iPod click wheel, supporting four distinct interaction modes simultaneously.
systems.detail: Touch: swipe gestures mapped to rotational input. Mouse: all buttons clickable + CTRL + drag for wheel navigation. Keyboard: arrow keys, Enter, Escape. Accessibility: full tab order and screen reader labels. A visual interaction guide documents all available controls.

systems.title: Spotify Integration
systems.summary: Full Spotify API integration — playlists, albums, artists, tracks, and playback control. Auth via Authorization Code Flow with PKCE.
systems.detail: No client secrets exposed. PKCE protects against CSRF and authorization injection. The app reads playlists, saved albums, saved artists, saved tracks, and user stats. Playback is transferred to and controlled from the browser. Each API response type (playlist, track, album, artist) required its own data-handling path due to structural differences.

systems.title: SVG Theming
systems.summary: The iPod shell is a scalable SVG. Dynamic overlays are generated by querying the SVG by element ID and extracting dimensions at runtime.
systems.detail: Theme switching updates the overlay without rebuilding the layout. The system works in both portrait and landscape orientations. Control positions remain accurate at any screen size because all coordinates are derived from the live SVG geometry, not hardcoded values.

cards.back: Component-based architecture enabling the complex navigation system. App Router used for page structure; all menu state is managed independently of the router.
Type-safe core components across navigation, data handling, and API integration. Strict types on all Spotify response shapes and menu state objects.
Combined for rapid style iteration. Tailwind for utility classes and layout; custom CSS for dynamic theming, SVG overlays, and click wheel animations.
Authorization Code Flow with PKCE extension. No client secrets stored or transmitted. Protects against CSRF and authorization injection attacks. Token refresh handled automatically.
Neon serverless PostgreSQL for storing user playlists and enabling social discovery features. Prisma as the type-safe ORM for all database interactions.

challenges.heading: Key Technical Challenges
challenges.intro: Main technical challenges solved across the navigation system, click wheel, and Spotify integration.
challenges.description: Each card summarises the problem and the approach — flip for the full story.

items.title: Custom navigation over dynamic API content
items.summary: Building a menu system that handles both static iPod menus and live Spotify API data with different structures per content type.
items.detail: Spotify returns playlists, tracks, albums, and artists in different shapes. Each view became its own edge case. The navigation system was built from scratch to assign state types to every menu item — distinguishing between opening a new view, triggering playback, or entering a nested menu. History is stored so back-navigation always works correctly, even mid-playback.

items.title: Translating physical interaction to digital
items.summary: A physical iPod gives tactile and audio feedback that a browser cannot replicate. The click wheel had to feel intuitive without any of that.
items.detail: Solved with a clear visual feedback system that signals every available interaction. Four input modes were implemented and tested independently: touch swipe, mouse drag with CTRL modifier, keyboard arrows, and full accessibility tab order with screen reader labels. An in-app instruction overlay documents all controls so users can discover every mode.

items.title: SVG overlay coordinate accuracy
items.summary: Dynamic overlays placed on top of the SVG shell had to remain pixel-accurate at any screen size and in both orientations.
items.detail: Rather than hardcoding positions, the system queries the live SVG DOM by element ID to read current dimensions and coordinates at runtime. Overlays are generated from these values. This means theme switching, orientation changes, and window resizing all recalculate correctly without any fixed coordinates in the code.

items.title: Mid-project architectural rebuild
items.summary: The project required a full restart halfway through due to early architectural decisions that made further development impractical.
items.detail: The rebuild forced rapid reprioritisation under time pressure. The key lesson: framework and routing decisions made at the start have outsized impact on long-term feasibility. The second architecture was designed with the navigation system as the primary constraint — everything else was built around it.

role.heading: Role
role.intro: Every part of SpotiPod was designed, built, and deployed by me — architecture, frontend, API integration, accessibility, and infrastructure.
role.title: Solo Developer
role.body: Full ownership of all systems: navigation architecture, click wheel implementation, Spotify OAuth and API integration, SVG theming, database design, and deployment.
lines: Multi-modal click wheel (touch, mouse, keyboard, a11y)
Spotify OAuth with PKCE — no client secrets
SVG shell with dynamic overlay theming
Neon PostgreSQL + Prisma for social features
Full accessibility — screen reader and tab navigation

deployment.heading: Deployment
deployment.intro: Deployed on Vercel with Neon serverless PostgreSQL. Spotify OAuth registered as a Spotify Developer app.

items.label: Frontend
items.detail: Next.js deployment with automatic CI from GitHub

items.label: Auth
items.detail: OAuth app registration, PKCE flow, token management
